title: Parosela purpusii
taxonomy: Parosela purpusii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Mexico. The plant is classified in the genus Parosela, which is part of the Rubiaceae family. Further classification includes the order Lamiales, class Magnoliopsida, and kingdom Plantae. This classification reflects the plant's botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
uses: Parosela purpusii is used in traditional medicine in some regions for its potential to treat various health conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

intro: Parosela purpusii, also known as Oreganillo or Mexican oreganillo, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Parosela purpusii include limonoids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Parosela purpusii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
